verdict: Architecturally sound proposal with verified audit data, pragmatic additive migration strategy, well-defined component boundaries, and mature risk management. All core claims verified against source code. Two non-blocking issues remain; neither justifies another review cycle. Ready for implementation.
summary:
This proposal (Revision 2) addresses a verified and significant gap: the CryptoSavingsTracker codebase has strong error-handling infrastructure (ServiceResult, ViewState, ErrorAwareViewModel, AsyncContentView, ErrorBannerView, ErrorStateView, FreshnessIndicatorView) that is overwhelmingly NOT wired into user-facing screens. The audit data is accurate -- I independently verified all 6 core claims: (1) AppError has exactly 25 cases in ErrorHandling.swift, (2) ErrorTranslator maps only 9/25 with a generic 'Something Went Wrong' default, (3) only 4/9 ViewModels (GoalViewModel, AssetViewModel, DashboardViewModel, GoalRowViewModel) conform to ErrorAwareViewModel while 5 do not, (4) GoalViewModel.calculateCurrentTotal() at line 99 catches exchange rate errors and calls `continue` -- silently producing $0.00 progress, (5) ErrorBannerView lines 82-98 use raw `.orange`/`.red` Color values instead of AccessibleColors tokens, and (6) AsyncContentView's degraded state (lines 32-48) renders a static HStack with no retry or dismiss.

The architecture plan is well-designed: (a) the additive ServiceResult migration (new *Result methods alongside existing async throws) avoids all protocol-conformance and mock breakage -- verified that no *Result methods or serviceError publishers currently exist on ExchangeRateServiceProtocol, CoinGeckoServiceProtocol, or BalanceServiceProtocol; (b) service-layer Combine publishers for error deduplication (Section 5.4.1) is the correct MVVM-aligned approach that avoids parent-child ViewModel coupling; (c) the DashboardErrorAggregator coordinator prevents DashboardViewModel from becoming a god object; (d) Phase 1.11 component validation tests before mass adoption is excellent risk mitigation; (e) Phase 3.5 validation spike provides an early architectural checkpoint; (f) the MVP cut-line at 32 hours with clear scope reduction is realistic.

The error routing contract (Section 5.1) cleanly resolves the ErrorHandler.shared dual-presentation hazard. I verified that no ErrorHandler.shared.handle() calls currently exist in ViewModels/ -- the contract starts from a clean baseline. The FreshnessPolicy injectable configuration (Section 5.2.1) aligns with existing DIContainer/Config.plist patterns. The Phase 0 baseline gate resolves the sole Run 2 blocker (PO-B-01). The 30 non-blocking issues and 26 suggestions from Run 2 are appropriately catalogued in Section 11 as implementation guidance.

I also verified that 7 screens already have .refreshable{} implementations (ContentView, PlanningView, GoalsListContainer, PlanHistoryDetailView, PlanHistoryListView, MonthlyExecutionView, MonthlyPlanningWidget), which Phase 4.15 should leverage rather than duplicate. CompactChartErrorView exists in only 2 files, confirming low blast radius for deprecation.

issues.title: ServiceResult default extension wrapper maps non-AppError exceptions to .networkUnavailable
issues.description: Phase 2's default protocol extension (backward-compatibility bridge) catches all non-AppError exceptions with `} catch { return .failure(.networkUnavailable) }`. This is semantically incorrect for local errors like SwiftData save failures or calculation overflows -- they would be misclassified as network errors. The extension is explicitly a temporary scaffold, but if any service relies on it in production rather than providing a proper override, error categorization degrades silently with no diagnostic signal.
issues.recommendation: Add `AppLog.warning("Non-AppError caught in ServiceResult bridge: \(error)", category: .validation)` in the generic catch branch. This surfaces adoption gaps during development without breaking production. Document in Phase 2 that every service MUST provide a concrete *Result implementation and the default extension is a temporary scaffold only.

issues.title: DashboardErrorAggregator ownership model unspecified
issues.description: Phase 3.7 introduces DashboardErrorAggregator as a coordinator that observes per-section error states and produces [UserFacingError] for CoalescedErrorBannerView. The proposal states it is 'independently testable' but does not specify its ownership model: child of DashboardViewModel, sibling in DIContainer, or SwiftUI @StateObject. This affects Combine subscription lifecycle, memory management, and testability surface. The DashboardViewModel already has 453 lines -- the aggregator must have a clear boundary.
issues.recommendation: Specify DashboardErrorAggregator as a stored property of DashboardViewModel, created in init. It observes the same service-layer publishers via Combine. Its sole output is a @Published [UserFacingError] array that DashboardView reads for CoalescedErrorBannerView. Lifecycle tied to ViewModel without DI overhead. Testable via direct instantiation with mock publishers.

non_blocking_issues.title: ServiceResult default extension wrapper maps non-AppError exceptions to .networkUnavailable
non_blocking_issues.reason: Temporary scaffold with a clear mitigation (AppLog.warning). Services are expected to provide concrete overrides. Risk is limited to development phase and is easily detectable.

non_blocking_issues.title: DashboardErrorAggregator ownership model unspecified
non_blocking_issues.reason: The correct answer is straightforward (child of DashboardViewModel). This is an implementation detail that does not affect the proposal's architectural validity.

suggestions.title: Leverage existing .refreshable implementations in Phase 4.15
suggestions.description: 7 screens already have .refreshable{} (ContentView, PlanningView, GoalsListContainer, PlanHistoryDetailView, PlanHistoryListView, MonthlyExecutionView, MonthlyPlanningWidget). Phase 4.15 should inventory these and wire them to ErrorAwareViewModel.retry() rather than adding redundant .refreshable modifiers. This reduces work and avoids duplicate refresh handlers.

suggestions.title: Add cancellation support to retry() protocol method
suggestions.description: ErrorAwareViewModel.retry() is `func retry() async` with no cancellation. Section 5.3.1 specifies a Cancel button after 5s timeout. Document that callers should wrap retry() in a cancellable Task and that ViewModel implementations should check Task.isCancelled during long operations.

suggestions.title: Consider optional degraded-view builder on AsyncContentView
suggestions.description: Section 5.9 hardwires degraded state to ErrorBannerView internally. MonthlyExecutionView's mid-flow error (Section 5.20) with cloud.slash badges may need custom degraded presentation. Consider an optional fourth ViewBuilder parameter with ErrorBannerView as default, enabling screen-specific customization without bypassing AsyncContentView.

suggestions.title: Define UserFacingError reassurance phrase rendering
suggestions.description: Appendix D messages include 'Your savings are safe' reassurance phrases. UserFacingError uses plain String. Consider adding `reassurancePhrase: String?` to UserFacingError, rendered in bold by ErrorBannerView/ErrorStateView. Simple, explicit, and improves financial trust UX.

assumptions.description: AppError has exactly 25 cases as enumerated in the proposal.
assumptions.method: Read Utilities/ErrorHandling.swift lines 15-49. Counted: 6 network + 5 API + 6 data + 4 calculation + 4 platform = 25.

assumptions.description: ErrorTranslator maps only 9/25 AppError cases; 16 fall to generic default.
assumptions.method: Read Utilities/ServiceResult.swift lines 133-215. Confirmed 9 explicit case matches before default clause.

assumptions.description: 4/9 ViewModels conform to ErrorAwareViewModel; 5 do not.
assumptions.method: Read all 9 ViewModel files. Conforming: GoalViewModel, AssetViewModel, DashboardViewModel, GoalRowViewModel. Non-conforming: MonthlyExecutionViewModel (raw error: Error?), MonthlyPlanningViewModel, GoalEditViewModel (validationErrors pattern), GoalDashboardViewModel, CurrencyViewModel (no error handling).

assumptions.description: GoalViewModel.calculateCurrentTotal() silently catches errors with `continue` at line 99.
assumptions.method: Read ViewModels/GoalViewModel.swift lines 93-100. Catch block logs via AppLog.error then calls continue, skipping allocation.

assumptions.description: ErrorBannerView uses raw Color values (.orange, .red) instead of AccessibleColors tokens.
assumptions.method: Read Views/Components/ErrorBannerView.swift lines 82-98. iconColor and backgroundColor switch on category using raw Color literals.

assumptions.description: AsyncContentView degraded state has no retry or dismiss affordance.
assumptions.method: Read Views/Components/AsyncContentView.swift lines 32-48. Renders HStack with warning icon and text only.

assumptions.description: No *Result methods or serviceError publishers exist on current service protocols.
assumptions.method: Read Services/Protocols/ServiceProtocols.swift. ExchangeRateServiceProtocol, CoinGeckoServiceProtocol, BalanceServiceProtocol have only async throws methods. No Combine publishers.

assumptions.description: No ErrorHandler.shared.handle() calls exist in ViewModel files currently.
assumptions.method: Grep for ErrorHandler.shared in ViewModels/ returned zero matches.

assumptions.description: OnboardingFlowView.createGoalFromTemplate() silently swallows errors, completing onboarding regardless.
assumptions.method: Read Views/Onboarding/OnboardingFlowView.swift lines 140-169. Catch block prints to console and calls onboardingManager.completeOnboarding().

assumptions.description: CurrencyViewModel has zero error handling -- no error property, no try/catch, no ErrorAwareViewModel conformance.
assumptions.method: Read ViewModels/CurrencyViewModel.swift. Only @Published properties are coins, coinInfos, supportedCurrencies, isLoading. No error state.

assumptions.description: GoalRowViewModel.hasError is declared but never set to true.
assumptions.method: Read ViewModels/GoalRowViewModel.swift. hasError initialized false at line 23, only reset to false at line 166. No code path sets it true.

assumptions.description: CompactChartErrorView exists in only 2 files -- low blast radius for deprecation.
assumptions.method: Grep for CompactChartErrorView found matches in ChartErrorView.swift (definition) and ChartErrorViewPreview.swift (preview).

assumptions.description: 7 screens already have .refreshable{} implementations that Phase 4.15 should leverage.
assumptions.method: Grep for .refreshable in Views/ found: ContentView, PlanningView, GoalsListContainer, PlanHistoryDetailView, PlanHistoryListView, MonthlyExecutionView, MonthlyPlanningWidget.

assumptions.description: The iOS app has no external package dependencies, confirming XCTest-native snapshot approach feasibility.
assumptions.method: Confirmed via CLAUDE.md: 'The iOS app has no external package dependencies.'
